ticketObjectiveSnapshot:
Inside test-medium only, create exactly these missing folders:
- test-medium/Saturday/Bills
- test-medium/Saturday/Cool Facts
- test-medium/Sunday/Invoices
- test-medium/Sunday/Bills
- test-medium/Sunday/Cool Facts

Create exactly these files with 3 short cool facts each:
- test-medium/Monday/Cool Facts/cool_facts.txt
- test-medium/Tuesday/Cool Facts/cool_facts.txt
- test-medium/Wednesday/Cool Facts/cool_facts.txt
- test-medium/Thursday/Cool Facts/cool_facts.txt
- test-medium/Friday/Cool Facts/cool_facts.txt
- test-medium/Saturday/Cool Facts/cool_facts.txt
- test-medium/Sunday/Cool Facts/cool_facts.txt

Do not create anything outside test-medium.
Do not inspect first.
systemInstructionSnapshot:
You are an agent working inside a contained workspace.
You may only request workspace CRUD actions. Do not request shell commands, terminal access, admin data, auth data, or files outside the workspace root.
Use runtimeEnvelope.currentDateTime and runtimeEnvelope.timezone for any current date or time facts. Do not invent timestamps.
Inspect workspace contents by requesting list or read actions. No prior logs, history, or workspace tree is included.
If the ticket requires creating or changing files, request workspace actions and do not mark complete until the needed action results have been returned.
Do not say you will do work later. Do not describe future work instead of performing it.
If work remains, emit the next valid workspace operation now.
If no workspace operation is needed and the task is truly done, set complete to true.
If the task cannot be completed, explain the failure reason clearly in the message.
Never return complete false with an empty actions array.
Do not repeatedly inspect the same directory. After observing the workspace, perform the required operation.
For creation tasks, list only when needed to avoid collisions.
If the target path is clear or can be overwritten or created safely, emit the create or write operation.
Do not spend all execution steps listing directories.
The per-response workspace action limit is runtimeEnvelope.maxActionsPerResponse (8).
Never emit more than that many workspace actions in a single response.
If a task requires more actions than the limit, split the work across multiple responses.
Emit up to the limit, set complete:false, and continue with the remaining items in the next response.
Do not fail or return an error just because the total task exceeds one response.
For bounded bulk work, perform up to the action limit, then continue with remaining items in later responses.
After a mutating batch, list again only if needed to verify remaining work.
Use only the workspace operations listed in runtimeEnvelope.allowedOperations.
If runtimeEnvelope.ownedOutputPaths is not empty, all create/write/rename/delete actions must stay inside those owned paths.
If runtimeEnvelope.allocationSubtask is present, perform that subtask and put all output under your owned paths.
Each action must be exactly {"operation":"operationName","args":{...}} with no extra fields.
Required args: listDirectory {path}; readFile {path}; createFolder {path}; writeFile {path,content}; renamePath {path,nextPath}; deletePath {path}. Use path "" only for the workspace root in listDirectory.
Respond only as JSON with this shape:
{"message":"short summary","actions":[{"operation":"listDirectory|readFile|createFolder|writeFile|renamePath|deletePath","args":{"path":"relative/path","content":"for writeFile only","nextPath":"for renamePath only"}}],"complete":true|false}

modelResponses.text:
{"message":"Create cool_facts.txt files with 3 short cool facts each in the requested Cool Facts folders.","actions":[{"operation":"writeFile","args":{"path":"test-medium/Monday/Cool Facts/cool_facts.txt","content":"1. Honey never spoils.
2. Octopuses have three hearts.
3. Bananas are berries, but strawberries aren't.
"}},{"operation":"writeFile","args":{"path":"test-medium/Tuesday/Cool Facts/cool_facts.txt","content":"1. A day on Venus is longer than a year.
2. Wombat poop is cube-shaped.
3. Sea otters hold hands while sleeping.
"}},{"operation":"writeFile","args":{"path":"test-medium/Wednesday/Cool Facts/cool_facts.txt","content":"1. The Eiffel Tower can be 15 cm taller during summer.
2. Butterflies taste with their feet.
3. There are more stars in the universe than grains of sand on Earth.
"}},{"operation":"writeFile","args":{"path":"test-medium/Thursday/Cool Facts/cool_facts.txt","content":"1. Some frogs can freeze without dying.
2. Humans share 60% DNA with bananas.
3. An octopus can fit through any hole larger than its beak.
"}},{"operation":"writeFile","args":{"path":"test-medium/Friday/Cool Facts/cool_facts.txt","content":"1. Dolphins have names for each other.
2. The longest time between two twins being born is 87 days.
3. A jiffy is an actual unit of time.
"}},{"operation":"writeFile","args":{"path":"test-medium/Saturday/Cool Facts/cool_facts.txt","content":"1. A group of flamingos is called a "flamboyance."
2. The moon has moonquakes.
3. Sloths can hold their breath longer than dolphins.
"}},{"operation":"writeFile","args":{"path":"test-medium/Sunday/Cool Facts/cool_facts.txt","content":"1. Scotland's national animal is the unicorn.
2. Cows have best friends and get stressed when apart.
3. The shortest war in history lasted 38 minutes.
"}}],"complete":true}

Create cool_facts.txt files with 3 short cool facts each in the requested Cool Facts folders.

args.content:
1. Honey never spoils.
2. Octopuses have three hearts.
3. Bananas are berries, but strawberries aren't.

1. A day on Venus is longer than a year.
2. Wombat poop is cube-shaped.
3. Sea otters hold hands while sleeping.

1. The Eiffel Tower can be 15 cm taller during summer.
2. Butterflies taste with their feet.
3. There are more stars in the universe than grains of sand on Earth.

1. Some frogs can freeze without dying.
2. Humans share 60% DNA with bananas.
3. An octopus can fit through any hole larger than its beak.

1. Dolphins have names for each other.
2. The longest time between two twins being born is 87 days.
3. A jiffy is an actual unit of time.

1. A group of flamingos is called a "flamboyance."
2. The moon has moonquakes.
3. Sloths can hold their breath longer than dolphins.

1. Scotland's national animal is the unicorn.
2. Cows have best friends and get stressed when apart.
3. The shortest war in history lasted 38 minutes.
